Fern PolrrrcAl Pnecrrcps CoMMrssroN
428 J Street r Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_2329
(9t6) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322_0886
April 8,2008
James R. Hammer
Deputy District Director -Administration
Department of Transportation, Caltrans
100 S. Main Street
Los Angeles, CA g}0l2
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-039
Dear Mr. Hammer:
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice regarding the post-governmental
restrictions provisions of the poritical Reform Act (the ..i.t..j r il;;;; 
"ylu. inqui.y i,
general in nature and does not involve a spercific governmental decision, rv" o." treating
yollr request as one for informal assistance.-
Please note that our advice is based solely on the Act. we therefore offer no
lPi{on-on the application, if any' of other post-government employment laws such as
Public contract code Section 1041I or on any incompatible activitles restrictions your
agency may impose under Government Code Section 19990. Additionally, this letter
should not be construed as advice on any conduct that may have already tat., pru...
This letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair political practices Commission
("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. tin ,"
Ogleshy (1975) I FppC Ops. 7t; Govt. Code section g3l 14.)
QUESTION
If you leave state service and are offered a position with a consulting firm, what
restrictions will be placed on your role in doing bu.siness with the o.pu.t.""nt or
Transportation ("Caltrans") and the statel
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through gl0l4. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulati.ns of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Seuions r8l r0 thr.i[i nggl of rirre 2 of rhe Caritirrnia
code of Regulations' AII regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Divisioi 6 of the Calitirrnia Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
r Infirrmal assistance dtres not prol'ide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
firrmal written advice. ( Regulation I 11329(c). enclosed. )
6:4


File No. I-08-039
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
The one-year ban would prohibit you from communicating with employees of
Caltrans, for one year after you leave this agency, if the communication is for the pLlrpose
of influencing any legislative or administrative action, or influencing any discretionary
act "involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license,
grant or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property." The permanent ban is a
lifetime ban that would prohibit you from participating in any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding in which you participated while a state administrative official at
Caltrans or any other state agency where you previously worked. In addition, you must
abide by restrictions on a public official who is negotiating or has any arrangement
concerning prospective employment. See discussion below.
FACTS
You are currently the Deputy District Director, Administration, for the
Department of Transportation ("Caltrans"). You have served in this capacity for the past
four years. In your position, you are responsible for managing the Office of Budgets,
Office of Business Management, Safety and Training, as well as responsible for idririrg
the Information Technology Unit. You have also assisted the Capitol Outlay program by
providing training in project management.
You are currently being recruited by an engineering consulting firm that wishes
for you to serve as a branch manager responsible for marketing and operations of their
Southern California office. The firm plans to apply for state architectural and
engineering contracts ("A&E contracts").
If you are offered a position with the consulting firm, you wish to know what
restrictions will be placed on your role in doing business with Caltrans and the state.
ANALYSIS
Post-Governmental Employment Restrictions
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types of post-
governmental employment provisions under the Act, colloquially known as the
"revolving door" prohibitions. In addition, Section 87407 prohibits certain state and local
officials from making, participating in making, or using their official position to influence
decisions affecting persons with whom they are negotiating employment, or have any
arrangement concerning employment. (Secti on 87 407 ; Regulation lg7 47 .)
one Year Ban: This ban prohibits a public official from appearing for
compensation before his or her former agency, or officer or employee thereof. for the
purpose of influencing any administrative, legislative or other specified action (including
contracts).


File No. I-08-039
Page No. 3
Permanent Ban: This ban prohibits a former state administrative official from
advising or representing any person, other than the State of California, for compensation
in any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding in which the official participated while
in state service. (See Sections 87401-874O2, Regulation 18741.1); and
Restrictions on Negotiating Prospective Employment: This prohibition restricts
public officials from negotiating or making any arrangement concerning prospective
employment (Section 87 407, Regulation 187 47).
The One-Year Ban
The one-year prohibition applies to employees who are designated or should be
designated in their former agency's conflict-of-interest code. As Deputy District
Director, Administration, for Caltrans, you are a designated employee. Therefore, for
one year after leaving Caltrans you may not communicate with your former agency in an
attempt to influence any transaction involving legislative or administrative action or other
specified action (including contracts).
Once you are no longer authorized to perform the duties of your job at Caltrans
and you stop performing them, the Act's one-year ban and the permanent ban will apply.
(Coler Advice Letter, No. I-07-089.)
Communications Covered bv the One-Year Ban
Not all communications to a former state administrative agency employer are
prohibited by the one-year ban. The ban extends only to those communications for the
purpose of influencing any legislative or administrative action, or influencing any
discretionary act "involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a
permit, license, grant or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property." (Section
87406(dX 1 ); Regulation 187 46.1 (b)(5).)
These communications include, but are not limited to, conversing directly or by
telephone, corresponding by writing or e-mail, attending a meeting, and delivering or
sending any communication. (Regulation I8746.2(a).) A communication is considered
to be for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action "if it is made for
the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifying, opposing,
delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding." (Regulation 15746.2(a).)
Therefore, you may not appear before or communicate with your former agency
regarding the issuance, amendment, or awarding of any A&E contracts for a period of
one year after you leave state service.


File No. I-08-039
Page No. 4
However, yoll may appear before or communicate with Caltrans for the pllrpose
of administering, implementing, or fulfilling the requirements of a pre-existing contract
or project, as long as the services do not involve the issuance, a*endment, awarding, or
revocation of a permit, license, agreement or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or
property. (Regulation 18746.1(b)(5XA); euiring Advice Leuer, No. A-03-272; Hanqn
Advice Letter, No. I-00-209.)
This is a fact-specific query. You may wish to submit more information about
and request further advice regarding a specific contract or project.
Certain other types of communications are not restricted under the one-year ban.
It is not considered a prohibited communication under the one-year ban, if an individual:
"(1) Participates as a panelist or formal speaker at a conference or
similar public event for educational purposes or to disseminate
research and the subject matter does not pertain to a specific action or
proceeding;
(2) Attends a general informational meeting, seminar, or similar event;
(3) Requests information concerning any matter of public record; or
(4) Communicares with the press," (Regulation lg746.Z(b)(l)_(4).)
Finally, the Commission has advised that a former agency official may draft
proposals on a client's behalf to be submitted to the agency as long as the former
employee is not identified in connection with the client's efforts to influence
administrative action. (CookAdvice Letter, No. 4-95-321; Harrison Advice Letter, No.
A-92-289.) Similarly, the ex-employee may use his or her expertise to advise clients on
the procedural requirements, plans, or policies of the employee's former agency so long
as the employee is not identified with the employer's efforts to influence tf,e agency.
(Perry Advice Letter, No. 4-94-004.)
ln summary, the one-year ban does not prevent you from requesting information
generally available to the public about agency business from Caltrarr o. oth". state
agencies. Nor does it prohibit you from attending informational meetings of Caltrans or
another state agency regarding existing laws, regulations, or policies, oJong as you do
not attempt to influence the agency's legislative or administrative action or fiom advising
your future employer or its clients on projects involving Caltrans, as long as you are not
identified in any communication with Caltrans and the action does not inlolve a
proceeding in which you participated while employed by Caltrans. Social conversations
with employees of Caltrans and other state agencies, not intended to influence
administrative or legislative action, also are not prohibited by the ban. (Tobias Advice
Letter, No. 4-96-089.) (Section 87a06(dXl).)


File No. I-08-039
Page No. 5
The Pennanent Ban
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding in which you participated while a state administrative official at
Caltrans or any other state agency. (Sections 8740I and 87402.) Thus, a public official
may never "switch sides" in a proceeding after leaving state service.
As a Deputy District Director for Administration at Caltrans, you are a state
administrative official for purposes of the Act. Ttrerefore you are subject to the
permanent ban once you leave state service. (Section 87400(b).) To determine if the
permanent ban applies to yollr situation, you would need to identify the proceedings in
which you participated while employed by the state.
You have not provided any facts, or details about specific proceedings that appear
to implicate this prohibition. Nevertheless, you should be aware of tt ir proiirion as it is
specifically applicable to any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedingi you may have
participated as Deputy District Director. "Judicial, quasi-judicial or other p.o.""ding',
means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other determinaiion, contract,
claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter
involving a specific party or parties in any court or state administrative agency...,,
(Section 87400(c).)
Ban on Influencing Prospective Employment
Section 87407, the ban against influencing prospective employment, provides:
"No public official, shall make, participate in making, or use his oi her official position to
influence, any governmental decision directly relating to any person with whom he or she
is negotiating, or has any arrangement concerning, prospectivi employment.,,
The term "public official" is defined, in part, in section gzo4g as ,.. . . every
member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local govemment agenc y ...,,
Caltrans is a state administrative agency, as defined in section 874OO (a), and as an
employee working for Caltrans, you are subject to this ban.
"A public official is 'negotiating' employment when he or she interviews or
discusses an offer of employment with an [potential] employer or his or her agent."
(Regulation 18747(c)(l).) The Commission has constmed the scheduling, coiduct, and
follow-up to an interview as one continuous process falling under the definition of
"negotiating" employment. (Bonner Advice Letter, No. I-9g-2g7.) However, the mere
act of sending a resume or application to a specific entity has not been considered
"negotiating." Similarly, entertaining informal inquiries about your future plans and
receiving expressions of general interest in discussing potential employment
opportunities at some point in the future is not considered ..negotiaiin g.,, (Id.)


File No. I-08-039
Page No. 6
"A public official has an 'arrangement' concerning prospective employment when
he or she accepts an employer's offer oi employment." (Regulaiion rc\a7iqb).)
You do not describe your contacts, if any, with the consulting firm with regard to
future employment. However, please note that ir yo, have sufficienlty discussed the
prospect of your future employment with the consulting firm to be considered parl of a
"negotiation," you would be prohibited, pursuant to Seition g74o7,from making,
participating in the making, or using your official position with caltrans to influence any
governmental decisions that impacts the consulting firm. If such is the case, yo, *outa
have to examine each decision you face and ur".rtoin if the consulting firm is involved in
or affected by that decision to determine if you are permitted to particlpate.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ER:jgl
Enclosure
